spans.quote: An attention function can be described as mapping a query and a set of key-value pairs to an output, where the query, keys, values, and output are all vectors.
spans.note: Canonical mechanism statement for attention in the Transformer paper.

quoted_definition: Attention maps a query and key-value pairs to an output vector by computing compatibility weights over the values.
local_context: The concept is a differentiable mechanism inside a neural sequence model, replacing recurrent/convolutional sequence alignment with learned weighted access over positions.

definition: A vector operation mapping query plus key-value pairs to an output vector by weighted combination.
causal_role: Selects which sequence positions contribute to each representation update.
Allows token representations to condition on other positions.
assumptions: Compatibility between queries and keys can be scored.
Values can be combined by learned weights.
scope: Neural sequence transduction and representation learning.
Applies to model internals, not human attention by default.
measurement_or_use: Operationalized through scaled dot-product attention and multi-head attention layers.
exclusions: Does not mean generic salience unless mapped to query-key-value weighting.
Does not by itself imply interpretability of human cognition.
downstream_implications: Sequence modeling can parallelize contextual access.
Long-range dependency handling becomes an architectural primitive.

known_limits: This block is a synthetic public-safe extraction, not a full literature review.
